preview_text:
The model favours Japan to win with a 69% probability, substantially ahead of Sweden's 8% chance. The model sees this as a heavily one-sided contest, with Japan the clear favourite on the neutral ground.

The win-draw-loss spread reflects that asymmetry. A Japan victory dominates the model's forecast, but a draw at 24% remains a meaningful outcome—more likely than a Swedish win. The match is not expected to be especially competitive; the model assigns Japan a commanding edge in the underlying match quality.

The model and market diverge sharply here. Sportsbooks and Polymarket both price Japan at roughly 45%, placing Sweden's chances at 27%—nearly triple the model's estimate. The gap of about 24 percentage points on the Japan win line is substantial. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources; bettors and forecast users should note the significant disagreement and form their own view on which is more reliable.
